question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - New hotline numbers dead (calls reach carrier/edge but never reach users): This aligns with an incident where incoming calls from a specific/new number range were received by the carrier/SBC but not forwarded to internal users because the SBC translation/routing table did not include the new range; the fix was to update translation/routing rules
context: SOURCE 8: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received Ticket number: TI-1612036 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 22 Owner: Employee 41 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.09.25 08:56 Closure Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again. Activities 18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22 Good morning We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us. Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds. Other external calls are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The problem has existed since last week. All employees are affected. 18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works. Test with number in specified range → call not delivered. In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown. 19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis Possible provider issue. Carrier contacted. 19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC. No routing errors visible on their side. 22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis For functioning numbers: INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams. For affected numbers: INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”. 22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer Good day We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC. However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally. Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions? Kind regards 22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers. The affected numbers belong to this new range. 23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review Comparison with document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3” Section regarding: • Normalization of incoming numbers • E.164 format • Translation rules on SBC • Voice routing policies Finding: New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table. Routing rule covers only the old number range. 24.09.25 – Test Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range. Test call performed. Call correctly signaled and delivered. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Internal documentation had not yet been updated. 26.09.25 – Adjustment Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Thank you for the support. 29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 22 The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Basel external calls dropping after ~30 seconds: This is the same symptom and root-cause pattern as ticket TI-1604879: calls were established but disconnected after ~30s due to a session timer / NAT session timeout mismatch introduced when a dedicated “Voice” VLAN and NAT policy were implemented; increasing NAT/session timeouts to match the architecture resolved the issue
context: SOURCE 16: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds Ticket number: TI-1604879 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 16 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.07.25 09:14 Closure Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52 Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable. Activities 22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk Good morning Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds. The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time. Internal Teams calls are not affected. Could you please review this? Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Could you please confirm: – Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls? – Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration? – Are all locations affected or only a specific one? Thank you very much. 22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response It only affects the Basel location. Both incoming and outgoing calls. The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier. Home office users are not affected. 22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. External call to mobile phone. Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds. No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.” 23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis Possible client error or unstable connection. Test with another device → same behavior. Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change. 23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs. Signaling is established correctly. The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client. Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location? Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Could this be relevant? 24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis SBC logs reviewed: – SIP INVITE successful – 200 OK correct – Call established – After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC No obvious error code. 24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis No blocking visible. Ports open according to standard configuration. 24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis Session-Expires header reviewed. Session timer set to 30 seconds. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. Test call performed. Call remains stable for several minutes. Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible. 28.07.25 – Coordination with network team New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Teams pop-up banners not shown on laptops (messages arrive only after opening Teams): This matches a reported issue where client banner notifications were disabled in Teams and Windows Focus Assist was active; after re-enabling banner notifications and disabling Focus Assist, pop-ups resumed
context: SOURCE 19: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Notifications not displayed Ticket number: TI-1615096 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 11 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Portal Type: Incident Created on: 08.10.25 08:41 Closure Closure date: 08.10.25 10:22 Closure text: Notifications active again. Root cause was a disabled Teams notification channel and Focus Assist. Steps performed according to Blueprint. Activities 08.10.25 08:41 Ticket received via portal. Description: A user no longer receives Teams pop-ups (chats/mentions). Messages are delivered but are only seen once Teams is opened. The issue has existed for “a few days.” 08.10.25 09:05 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 08.10.25 09:20 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Is the issue only occurring on the laptop or also on the smartphone? And was Windows recently updated? Thank you. 08.10.25 09:40 – Customer response Only on the laptop. Windows Update ran last week. 08.10.25 10:05 – Internal note Remote check with the user. Teams notifications within Teams itself are disabled (banner). Additionally, Windows Focus Assist is active and blocking pop-ups. According to “Blueprint – Teams Notifications Standard – Version 1.1”, the standard checks were performed: Teams notification settings, Windows Notification Center, Focus Assist, Teams restart. 08.10.25 10:15 – Customer feedback Pop-ups are displayed again. Mentions work immediately. 08.10.25 10:22 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 11 Notifications were blocked both in Teams and by Windows Focus Assist. After adjusting the settings according to the Blueprint, pop-ups are displayed again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

Activities 18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22 Good morning We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us. Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The problem has existed since last week. All employees are affected. 18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works. Test with number in specified range → call not delivered. In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown. 19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis Possible provider issue. Carrier contacted. 19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC. No routing errors visible on their side. 22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis For functioning numbers: INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams. For affected numbers: INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”. 22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer Good day We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC. However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally. Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions? Kind regards 22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers. The affected numbers belong to this new range. 23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review Comparison with document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3” Section regarding: • Normalization of incoming numbers • E.164 format • Translation rules on SBC • Voice routing policies Finding: New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table. Routing rule covers only the old number range. 24.09.25 – Test Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range. Test call performed. Call correctly signaled and delivered. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Activities 18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22 Good morning We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us. Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds. Other external calls are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The affected numbers belong to this new range. 23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review Comparison with document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3” Section regarding: • Normalization of incoming numbers • E.164 format • Translation rules on SBC • Voice routing policies Finding: New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table. Routing rule covers only the old number range. 24.09.25 – Test Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range. Test call performed. Call correctly signaled and delivered. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Thank you for the support. 29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 22 The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
